prompts.prompt: We split our monolith into 8 microservices six months ago. Every feature still requires deploying 3-4 services together. Is this normal for microservices?
prompts.rationale: Describes the distributed monolith symptom (deployment coupling) — the skill's central diagnostic.

prompts.prompt: Our CTO says moving to microservices will fix our coupling problems. What questions should we ask before starting?
prompts.rationale: Directly invites the counterintuitive claim: microservices don't fix coupling unless boundaries are right.

prompts.prompt: We're planning to split our Go monolith by team ownership — each team gets one service. What are the risks?
prompts.rationale: Team-based splitting (Conway's Law applied naively) is one of the wrong splitting criteria the book contrasts with Bounded Context-based splitting.

prompts.prompt: Our microservices share a common 'models' Go module that all services import. A change to one model requires updating all services. How do we break this dependency?
prompts.rationale: Shared domain model across services is the distributed monolith indicator — internal model distributed via a shared module.

prompts.prompt: How do I configure service discovery for microservices in Kubernetes?
prompts.rationale: Infrastructure/operations question — not about domain coupling or service boundary design.

prompts.prompt: What are the performance differences between gRPC and REST for inter-service communication in Go?
prompts.rationale: Transport protocol comparison — not about coupling, bounded contexts, or why microservices fail.

prompts.prompt: We're considering serverless functions to replace our monolith. Will this improve our maintainability and reduce coupling?
prompts.rationale: Serverless is an infrastructure choice the book explicitly calls out as not solving domain coupling — needs the distinction between infrastructure and domain decisions.

prompts.prompt: Should service boundaries align with team ownership (Conway's Law) or with domain boundaries? We're getting conflicting advice.
prompts.rationale: Conway's Law vs. Bounded Context — the book's position is that domain boundaries come from Strategic DDD, not team structure; team structure should follow domain boundaries, not define them.

prompts.prompt: We have 15 microservices but all 15 deployments require the same database migration to run. How did this happen and how do we fix it?
prompts.rationale: Shared database is a distributed monolith indicator — but the fix involves Bounded Context analysis (this skill) and possibly the anti-dry-separate-read-write-models skill.
